medical_history.chief_complaint: continuous cough since discharged home with epigastric and back pain with deep breath
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Patient is a female with a history of Marfan Syndrome who is s/p repair of type A dissection with aortic arch replacement. She presented with shortness of breath, and was found to have large left effusion and pericardial effusion. After treatment, she was re-admitted with cough, epigastric and back pain, increasing SOB and weakness.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Aortic aneurysm, Aortic dissection, Marfan's syndrome, Bilateral retinal detachments. Surgical history includes bilateral eye surgery for retinal detachments and C-section.
medical_history.family_history: No known thrombophilic or bleeding disorders.

physical_examination.specialty_examination: General: Weight changes. Respiratory: Cough constant since discharge and worse with lying supine. GI: Nausea past 24 hours with coughing.
